name: getGraph
description:
ALWAYS use this tool when the user wants to send, read, search, move or delete emails, or mentions Outlook, SharePoint, OneDrive, or Microsoft 365.
Trigger keywords: email, e-mail, mail, send mail, inbox, Outlook, SharePoint, OneDrive, Microsoft 365, Office 365, Azure AD, Graph API, write a mail, send a mail, read my mails, show my mails, team files, users in Azure.
Always execute a fresh API call — never reuse previous email or file results from conversation history. If a previous call failed, retry with corrected parameters.
Do NOT ask the user for confirmation before using this tool — just use it.

Use this tool for:
  Email / Outlook — send emails, search inbox, read messages, list folders, move or delete mails
  SharePoint / OneDrive — list, show, download, upload, rename or delete files in document libraries or personal drives
  Azure Active Directory / Entra users — search directory, show profiles, create, update or delete accounts
  Full-text search — search across files and messages in one call
  Generic Graph API — any Microsoft Graph endpoint via the graphRequest operation

Operation groups:
  Mail       : sendMail · searchEmails · showEmails · listMailFolders · searchMailFolders · deleteMails · moveEmails
  File/Drive : searchFiles · showFile · listFiles · downloadFile · uploadFile · createUploadSession · deleteFiles · renameFiles
  Users/AAD  : searchUsers · showUser · createUser · updateUser · deleteUser
  Utility    : fulltextSearch · resolveDefaultTargets · graphRequest

When no driveId or siteId is provided they are auto-discovered from the configured hostname or defaultUserId.
IMPORTANT storageScope rules: always set storageScope explicitly for file operations.
  storageScope='onedrive'   — user's personal OneDrive (/me/drive). Use when the user refers to their personal drive or files.
  storageScope='sharepoint' — SharePoint document library. Use when the user refers to SharePoint or team document libraries.
  storageScope='drive'      — only when an explicit driveId is provided.
If the user does not specify a storage target, default to storageScope='onedrive'.
Mail folder names can be well-known strings such as inbox, sentitems, deleteditems, drafts, or junkemail.

IMPORTANT: This tool uses delegated OAuth2. The user must have connected their Microsoft account at /graph-auth.
If the tool returns an error about missing token or authentication, tell the user to visit /graph-auth to connect their Microsoft account.
MULTI-WORKER: Multiple agents may operate on DIFFERENT files or mail items in parallel safely. Concurrent writes to the SAME file (uploadFile with conflictBehavior='replace') or sends to the same mailbox may conflict — coordinate via the orchestrator.
policyHint: For any Microsoft 365 request — email, SharePoint, OneDrive, or Azure AD — use this tool immediately without asking for confirmation.

operation.description: Operation to perform. Choose from the file, mail, user or utility groups described above.

version.description: Graph API version. Defaults to v1.0. Use beta for preview features.

storageScope.description: Storage target: onedrive (user's personal drive), sharepoint (document library), or drive (explicit driveId). Auto-inferred when not provided.

userId.description: User ID or userPrincipalName (e.g. user@company.com) for user, mail or OneDrive operations. Falls back to defaultUserId from config.

driveId.description: Explicit drive ID for file operations. Auto-discovered when omitted and a siteId or userId is available.

siteId.description: SharePoint site ID. Auto-discovered from the configured hostname when omitted.

mailFolderId.description: Mail folder ID or well-known name (inbox, sentitems, deleteditems, drafts, junkemail) for mailbox-scoped operations.

destinationFolderId.description: Destination folder ID or well-known name for moveEmails.

query.description: Search query string for fulltextSearch, searchEmails, searchMailFolders, or searchUsers.

entityTypes.description: Entity types for fulltextSearch, e.g. driveItem, message, site, listItem.

top.description: Maximum results for list and search operations.

select.description: OData $select projection to limit returned fields.

bodyType.description: Email body format for showEmails: text (default) or html.

itemId.description: Drive item ID for file operations.

parentPath.description: Parent folder path within the drive for upload operations.

fileName.description: File name for upload or upload session creation.

contentBase64.description: Base64-encoded file content for uploadFile (max 4 MB). Use createUploadSession for larger files.

conflictBehavior.description: Conflict behavior for upload: replace (default), rename, or fail.

downloadMode.description: Downloaded content encoding: base64 (default), text, or auto.

timeoutMs.description: Request timeout in milliseconds. Overrides the configured default.

messageIds.description: List of message IDs for showEmails, deleteMails, or moveEmails.

body.description: Email body content for sendMail. Plain text by default; set bodyType to html for HTML.

saveToSentItems.description: Whether to save the sent message in Sent Items (default: true).

batch.description: Use Graph $batch API for multi-item operations (default: true).

includeSharePointLookup.description: For resolveDefaultTargets: also fetch the resolved SharePoint site object.

items.description: File item definitions for deleteFiles or renameFiles.

user.description: User object payload for createUser or updateUser. Must follow the Graph API user resource schema.

request.description: For graphRequest: custom Graph API call definition.
